prerequisites: A Front account with admin access. You must be a Front company admin to create or manage API tokens.
An API token generated in Front to authenticate requests, which maps to the required api_key field.
A start date, because the sync start_date config field is required to bound the initial data extraction.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: The Front Core API uses Bearer tokens. The connector sends the api_key in the Authorization header as 'Bearer <token>' on every request, matching the connector's required api_key secret field.

setupSteps.title: Create a Front API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Front as a company admin, go to Settings > Developers, open the API Tokens tab, and click Create API token. You must be an admin to create or manage API tokens. Define the access permission scope for the token when you create it, then store the generated token securely.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API token
setupSteps.body: Enter the Front API token in the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends it as 'Bearer <token>' in the Authorization header on every API request.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync window and page size
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to control how far back data is extracted. Optionally set page_limit to control the number of records returned per API page during pagination.

sources.title: Front API - Create and revoke API tokens
Front Help Center - How to create and revoke API tokens
